content.problemStatement:
**This is the easy version of the problem. Here yout task is to find the minimum possible path sum.**

Given a binary sequence AAA of length NNN, we construct an N√óNN \times NN√óN matrix BBB as follows:


If i‚Č§ji \leq ji‚Č§j, then Bi,j=‚ąĎk=ijAkB_{i,j} = \sum_{k=i}^j A_kBi,j‚Äč=‚ąĎk=ij‚ÄčAk‚Äč.
Otherwise, Bi,j=Bj,iB_{i,j} = B_{j,i}Bi,j‚Äč=Bj,i‚Äč.
Define a path from (1,1)(1,1)(1,1) to (N,N)(N, N)(N,N) as a sequence of pairs:

P=[(i1,j1),(i2,j2),‚Ä¶,(i2N‚ąí1,j2N‚ąí1)],P = [(i_1,j_1), (i_2,j_2), \dots, (i_{2N-1},j_{2N-1})],P=[(i1‚Äč,j1‚Äč),(i2‚Äč,j2‚Äč),‚Ä¶,(i2N‚ąí1‚Äč,j2N‚ąí1‚Äč)],where:


(i1,j1)=(1,1)(i_1, j_1) = (1, 1)(i1‚Äč,j1‚Äč)=(1,1) and (i2N‚ąí1,j2N‚ąí1)=(N,N)(i_{2N-1}, j_{2N-1}) = (N, N)(i2N‚ąí1‚Äč,j2N‚ąí1‚Äč)=(N,N).
(ik+1,jk+1)=(ik+1,jk)(i_{k+1}, j_{k+1}) = (i_k + 1, j_k)(ik+1‚Äč,jk+1‚Äč)=(ik‚Äč+1,jk‚Äč) or (ik,jk+1)(i_k, j_k + 1)(ik‚Äč,jk‚Äč+1) for 1‚Č§k<2N‚ąí11 \leq k < 2N - 11‚Č§k<2N‚ąí1.
The path sum is defined as ‚ąĎ(i,j)‚ąąPBi,j\sum_{(i,j) \in P} B_{i,j}‚ąĎ(i,j)‚ąąP‚ÄčBi,j‚Äč.
Your task is to find the **minimum possible path sum**.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN.
The second line contains a binary string AAA of length NNN.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line: the **minimum possible path sum**.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10610^6106.
